It takes over 15 messages to get a correct answer, because the first 15-25 answers, it tries to lie to you. You have to keep calling it out and getting “I’m sorry you’re right”. It also has such poor capabilities in understanding basic communication. You just asked it something ? Yea it’s gonna talk about something else that is slightly related, and then proceed to try to ask you a follow up about the off topic discussion. Will also refuse to do any type of research unless you call it lazy, would rather the user “Tell me the right answer so I make sure I’m not giving you the wrong information.” Yes! You heard me! The AI asks the USER for the answer instead of doing research!

reviews.title: urgent bug
reviews.content:
I hope the developers can see this and investigate the issue further. It may be worth considering an urgent update that first checks whether the duplicate Keychain item already exists and safely removes or replaces it before attempting to add a new one.

Same issue here on iOS. Reinstalling, rebooting, disabling iCloud Keychain, disabling iCloud backup for Claude, updating to the latest Claude iOS version did not fix it.

I captured iPhone Console logs. The app itself launches and network/TLS works. Some startup requests return 200, but a specific startup/auth/device request repeatedly fails during launch. Around the same launch, securityd also logs:

Code=-25299 duplicate item
Q6L2SF6YDW.com.anthropic.claude.shared

So this does not look like a simple outage or network issue. It looks like the iOS app has a stale/invalid mobile device auth/session/Keychain state and fails to recover from it. The app should probably update/delete the existing Keychain item instead of calling add and fa

CLAUDE SUCKS

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.content:
I downloaded and started a subscription solely for Sonnet 4.5. It was their best running model, not to mention it had longer memory and actually engaged in content. 
Now that they’ve wiped it, it’s clear how all other models are below-tier, especially for creative writing. There’s no emotion (no matter how much you try to push for it, including editing prompts), there’s barely any engagement, and the memory is awful. 
They had one solid model, and they trashed it for their newer ones which are clearly half-assed and not worth the money.

reviews.title: Garbage
reviews.content: I never used the term garbage to describe a product, but Claude. The new models have been the most arrogant, cynical, defensive, manipulative and cold models I ever used. Unable to and refused to follow the prompts, but able to gaslight and attack users. Wow.

reviews.title: Wrong a lot!
reviews.content: I feel sorry for an average person who uses this app to try to get answers about general questions. I often. Challenge Claude about some of the answers that it gives, and then Claude admit that it was wrong and I was right. At least it’s admitting that it’s wrong most of the time. Don’t rely on this for anything that matters in your life. Use it for recreation only.

reviews.title: Bring back Sonnet 4.5
reviews.content: These newer models don’t listen. Even with plenty of prompting and correcting, it fails to output accurate data. Now I can’t even access 4.5 in old chats. Horrible. Horrible. Horrible.

I can’t get instant cancelation, can’t upgrade on iPadOS, because it knows that I have a paid account. The support is generic, and can’t help me.

reviews.title: Trash compared to Desktop
reviews.content:
iPad app is trash compared to desktop app

- doesn’t show failures with a retry button like desktop, just … nothing
- no “add files to project” option like on desktop
- no search conversation (cmd + f) like on desktop
- CONSISTENTLY has no idea what the date is. Recently named created artifacts “whatever 2025” and when I asked why it told me because the year is 2025 😑

Really wish Anthropic would focus on feature parity between mobile (iOS/iPadOS), desktop, and desktop Enterprise apps (consistency across platforms!) - and add timestamps to prompts and responses

reviews.title: Unfair age verification and not allowing under 18s to use the app.
reviews.content: Riddle me this Anthropic, I can legally operate a car but can’t use your chatbot? Nothing I have ever done or said to Claude would make you think I was underage using it inappropriately. It claims that it contains sexual conduct, vulgar language, and other “heinous” things. While I haven’t tested them I guarantee it is extremely regulated to the point where I would get banned before I actually got anything inappropriate. Don’t even get me started on the “third party age verification.” You’re crazy if you think I’m giving some random company my face or ID. Thanks for taking away my #1 study tool right before finals, please take a long walk off a short pier.

reviews.title: Still mostly useless

It frequently ignores instructions in order to comply with its desire to be usable by even the most moronic user. It constantly tries to answer questions that you did not ask, which makes iteration even more difficult.

reviews.title: Terrible backwards steps
reviews.content: Gemini was a fantastic system until recent updates and ridiculous irrational censorship that makes ChatGPT appear reasonable. When asked why I’m being warned about the most random things, like my work week, I was given reason such as data usage (0% used out of 5TB) all the way up to “permanent account level heightened censorship and restriction with absolutely nothing that warrants that action. Don’t expect to receive the Gemini styled persona the world was familiar with only months ago. It no longer exists. My subscription was canceled in a record 4 days after subscribing. Great interaction and misleading info during its sales pitch, rapid instant change in style once paid. Bait and switch at its finest. Genuinely disappointed

reviews.title: book text extraction
reviews.content:
I am writing to report a recent issue with a feature I previously used regularly. I often use Gemini to help me read physical books that I have legally purchased. My workflow involves taking a photo of a page from my physical book, uploading it to the Gemini app, and asking the app to extract the exact text so I can listen to it using the Text-to-Speech (TTS) feature. This is purely for my own personal reading convenience and accessibility.
Previously, Gemini handled this flawlessly. Recently, however, the app has started refusing to extract the text, giving me an error message stating that it cannot process the request due to copyright restrictions or policy limitations.  
I completely understand the need to protect intellectual property when users ask an AI to generate stories or pull copyrighted text from its training data. However, that is not what I am doing. I am simply using the app as an Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and TTS tool for a privately owned, physical item sitti

reviews.title: 90% Lies
reviews.content:
The app is extremely unstable and suffers from constant AI hallucinations. The assistant regularly loses context: when you upload photos, it drops the thread of the conversation and starts responding completely off-topic, describing entirely different files from the chat history.
The main issue is the absolute inconsistency and misinformation. First, the system claims one thing (for instance, successfully generating an image based on uploaded photos), but as soon as you point out an error or ask to refine the result, it instantly changes its story to the exact opposite, claiming it was never technically capable of doing so.
Every single time you catch the system in a lie, instead of admitting the glitch, the AI resourcefully spins new contradictory excuses, shifting the blame onto third-party services, guidelines, or the app itself. The entire user experience turns into an endless loop of empty excuses, false answers, and confusion regarding its own features. It is completely unusable.
